28 January 2026 Military News

Operations
Defense Policy / Programs
Defense Industry
Other Conflicts
News Reports

Current Operations

  • Venezuela's Rodríguez rejects US push to cut ties with China, Russia, Iran: US Intel. Press TV 28 Jan 2026 -- US intelligence reports indicate that Venezuela's interim president, Delcy Rodríguez, is resisting Washington's push to realign the country's foreign partnerships.

Defense Policy / Programs

  • Army modernizes munitions production with landmark lease at Pine Bluff Arsenal US Army 28 Jan 2026 -- In a major step to modernize the U.S. munitions supply chain and enhance warfighter lethality, the Department of the Army is awarding a lease to Hanwha Defense USA for a landmark enhanced use lease, or EUL, to build and operate a new munitions facility at Pine Bluff Arsenal, Arkansas.
  • Army nears 100,000 Wickr users as secure communications platform expands across the force US Army 28 Jan 2026 -- The U.S. Army is approaching a major milestone as it nears 100,000 registered users on Wickr, a secure communications platform designed to meet some of the military's most stringent operational requirements. The rapid growth reflects the Army's continued commitment to strengthening secure, agile and resilient information exchange across the force.
  • National Guard Repair Depot Key to Army Aircraft Maintenance DoD News 28 Jan 2026 -- At the Connecticut National Guard's 1109th Aviation Classification and Repair Depot, workers refurbish and maintain the Army's fleet of rotary-wing aircraft. This unique job can save the War Department millions of dollars a year.
  • US, Japan refine interoperability during exercise North Wind 26 US Air Force 28 Jan 2026 -- Members of the 374th Airlift Wing integrated with U.S. Army and Japan Ground Self-Defense Force personnel to execute a joint forcible entry operation during exercise North Wind 26 in Hokkaido, Jan. 22.
  • Under Secretary of the Air Force visits Indo-Pacific to engage with Airmen, allies US Air Force 28 Jan 2026 -- Under Secretary of the Air Force Matt Lohmeier arrived at Joint Base Pearl Harbor-Hickam Jan. 24 to kick off his tour of the Pacific Air Force's area of responsibility and meet with Airmen and Guardians throughout the region.
  • Upcoming Artemis II Space Mission Has Multiple Yuma Proving Ground Connections DoD News 28 Jan 2026 -- NASA announced the first crewed mission of the Artemis II will fly around the moon after an opportune launch window sometime between early February and the end of April.
  • Massachusetts National Guard leaders deepen partnership with Paraguay Army News 28 Jan 2026 -- Maj. Gen. Gary W. Keefe, the adjutant general of the Massachusetts National Guard, led a series of senior leader engagements in Asunción from Jan. 16-22 to strengthen bilateral defense cooperation between Massachusetts and Paraguay.
  • JTF-Bravo Aircraft Support U.S.- Panama Effort to Strengthen Interoperability in Jungle Training SOUTHCOM 28 Jan 2026
  • 571st MSAS Advances Costa Rican Aviation Capabilities SOUTHCOM 28 Jan 2026 -- Air Advisors from the 571st Mobility Support Advisory Squadron (MSAS) recently completed a multi-week engagement with the Costa Rican Servicio de Vigilancia Aérea (SVA), strengthening the nation's aviation maintenance, flight safety, logistics, Intelligence, Surveillance, and Reconnaissance (ISR), and base defense capabilities under the U.S. Southern Command Theater Maintenance Partnership Initiative (TMPI) program.
  • 2nd Marine Logistics Group 3D printed Drone USMC News 28 Jan 2026 -- Amidst the Department of War's "Drone Dominance" intent to acquire 300,000 one-way attack drones by calendar year 2028, 2nd Marine Logistics Group has developed the Marine Corps' first National Defense Authorizing Act-compliant 3D printed drone. The drone, titled HANX, is a holistically adaptable approved platform that can be tailored to the needs of the warfighter.

  • NATO Secretary General joins Industry during NATO's C-UAS Week NATO 28 Jan 2026 -- On Wednesday (28 January), NATO Secretary General Mark Rutte joined an Industry Day during NATO's Counter-Uncrewed Autonomous Systems (C-UAS) Week at NATO Headquarters in Brussels.
  • Zakharova Exposes NATO's Blind Spot: Bloc Powerless to Counter US on Greenland Sputnik 28 Jan 2026 -- NATO countries have no means to confront the United States if it decides to seize Greenland, as the bloc does not provide for mechanisms to resolve such issues, Russian Foreign Ministry spokeswoman Maria Zakharova said on Wednesday.

  • 2026 financial declaration campaign: which categories of service members are required to file declarations Ukraine MOD 28 Jan 2026
  • Government enables faster fortification construction by removing restrictions for the State Special Transport Service Ukraine MOD 28 Jan 2026
  • Mykhailo Fedorov announces appointment of Yevhenii Khlebnikov to lead lower-tier air defense Ukraine MOD 28 Jan 2026

  • A new age of airspace authority begins at Tindal Australia DOD 28 Jan 2026 -- Assistant Minister for Defence, Peter Khalil, will today officially open the new MQ-4C Triton facilities at RAAF Base Tindal in the Northern Territory, marking a major milestone for the Australian Defence Force (ADF) and delivering a significant boost to Australia's air and maritime surveillance capabilities.
  • Huge jobs boost for Western Sydney with $500M investment from Albanese Government Australia DOD 28 Jan 2026 -- More than 2,000 jobs will be created in Western Sydney following a $508 million investment from the Albanese Government in two major capital works projects that are critical to Australia's national security.

  • DPRK Missile Administration Test-fires Large-caliber MRLS KCNA 28 Jan 2026 -- The Missile Administration of the DPRK conducted a test-fire on Jan. 27 to verify the effectiveness of a renewed large-caliber multiple rocket launcher system which employed new technology.
  • Statement on Democratic People's Republic of Korea ballistic missile launches Australia DFAT 28 Jan 2026
  • We have noted parties concerned have different views regarding launched objects' definition, FM comments on North Korea's reported ballistic missiles launch Global Times 28 Jan 2026

  • Israel to enter talks with US on new 10-year 'security deal': Report Press TV 28 Jan 2026 -- Israel is reportedly preparing to begin negotiations with the United States on a new 10-year "security agreement" aimed at extending Washington's military support, with a greater focus on joint projects rather than direct financial assistance.

  • Signing of the Agreement between the Government of Japan and the Government of Canada concerning the Transfer of Defense Equipment and Technology Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 28 Jan 2026

  • Under Secretary of War for Policy Elbridge Colby's visit to MOD Ministry of Defense of Japan 28 Jan 2026
  • Courtesy Call on Vice-Minister for Foreign Affairs FUNAKOSHI Takehiro by U.S. Under Secretary of War Elbridge Colby Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 28 Jan 2026

  • Israel MOD Marks Milestone with Rollout of 1,000th "David" Armored Vehicle Israel MOD 28 Jan 2026 -- The Israel Ministry of Defense (IMOD) Mission to the United States marked a significant milestone with the rollout of the 1,000th "David" armored 4x4 vehicle, produced by MDT Armor in the United States.
  • Israel MOD Lead Tour of Advanced Defense Technologies for Hungarian Defense Minister Israel MOD 28 Jan 2026 -- The Israel Ministry of Defense (IMOD)'s International Defense Cooperation Directorate (SIBAT) led an official tour this week showcasing advanced Israeli Defense-Tech solutions for Hungary's Minister of Defense, Kristóf Szalay-Bobrovniczky, as part of his official visit to Israel.

  • Minister Pollard speech at ADS Annual Dinner 2026 UK MOD 28 Jan 2026 -- Minister for Defence Readiness and Industry, Luke Pollard MP, delivered a keynote speech at the ADS Annual Dinner.

  • G7 summit in Evian: Armed Forces support service and restriction on the use of airspace Federal Council - Swiss Government 28 Jan 2026 -- At its meeting on 28 January, the Federal Council approved the deployment of the Armed Forces in a supporting role to assist the civilian authorities with security measures during the G7 summit to be held in Évian-les-Bains, France, from 15 to 17 June. This deployment, which is subsidiary in nature, complements the security measures put in place by the civilian authorities. Parliament will vote on the deployment at its next session in March.

  • Time to think as Europeans, industry urges EDA conference European Defence Agency 28 Jan 2026 -- A "European preference" in EU defence procurement is necessary and should not be seen as protectionism, Saab President and Chief Executive Micael Johansson told the European Defence Agency Annual Conference on Tuesday.
  • Kallas presses EDA to move from support to leadership in EU defence European Defence Agency 28 Jan 2026 -- The European Defence Agency (EDA) must go beyond its role as a supporter of EU cooperation to a leadership role, especially in innovation, Head of EDA and High Representative Kaja Kallas, told the Agency's 2026 Annual Conference.
  • HR/VP Kaja Kallas: Keynote speech at the European Defence Agency Annual Conference 2026 European External Action Service (EEAS) 28 Jan 2026 -- Dear colleagues,

  • Working group proposes that real estate expropriation for security reasons be assigned to Ministry of Defence Finnish MoD 28 Jan 2026 -- On 10 December 2024, the Finnish Ministry of Defence set up a project to examine the need to amend the Act on the Expropriation of Immovable Property and Special Rights to Ensure National Security and related legislation. The objective was to draw an overview of how the current legislation should be amended to ensure fair and effective safeguarding of real estate critical to national security.

  • Deputy prime minister W. Kosiniak-Kamysz: we will always watch over building strength, security and peace Poland - Ministry of National Defence 28 Jan 2026 -- On 28 January 2026, the minister of national defence has met in Warsaw with the members of the military diplomatic corps accredited in Poland.

  • B. Braže with Minister of State of the United Kingdom S. Doughty: All allies must continue to support Ukraine's self-defence and maximise pressure on Russia. We highly appreciate the UK's contribution to the security of the Baltic and NATO's eastern flank Latvia MFA 28 Jan 2026 -- On 28 January 2026, the Minister of Foreign Affairs, Baiba Braže, welcomed Stephen Doughty, Minister of State for Europe, North America and Overseas Territories of the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland, on a working visit.
  • B. Braže with Swedish Foreign Minister M. M. Stenergard: Our countries share common goals - to strengthen regional security and defence, and to achieve lasting peace in Ukraine. We highly appreciate deployment of Swedish troops in NATO brigade in Latvia Latvia MFA 28 Jan 2026 -- On 28 January 2026, the Minister of Foreign Affairs, Baiba Braže, welcomed the Minister for Foreign Affairs of the Kingdom of Sweden, Maria Malmer Stenergard, on a working visit.
  • European Commision approves further advancement of Latvia's SAFE application for defence funding Latvia MOD 28 Jan 2026 -- The European Commission has approved funding requests for the second group of national defense plans under the Security Action Fund for Europe (SAFE), marking another significant step in strengthening the security of the European Union.

  • Sweden Negotiating 'Nuclear Umbrella' Protection With UK, France - Prime Minister Sputnik 28 Jan 2026 -- Swedish Prime Minister Ulf Kristersson said that the country is in talks with the UK and France about protecting the kingdom under their "nuclear umbrella."

  • A look at the Defence news 19 - 25 January Netherlands Ministry of Defence 28 Jan 2026

  • Romanian-German ministerial dialogue in the field of defense Romania - Ministry of National Defence 28 Jan 2026 -- The minister of national defense, Radu Miruță, had on Wednesday, January 28, a bilateral meeting with his German counterpart, Boris Pistorius, in the context of the official visit of the Romanian governmental delegation, led by the Prime Minister of Romania, to Berlin.
  • Merz recognises Romania's contribution to European security Germany - Federal Government 28 Jan 2026 -- Press conference with Chancellor Merz and Romania's Prime Minister Bolojan

  • HoPH eyes more drills with Australian Navy on rules-based maritime order Philippine News Agency 28 Jan 2026 -- The Philippine Navy (PN) is looking to have more engagements with the Royal Australian Navy (RAN) as it goes about its goal of maintaining a "secure and rules-based maritime order."
  • AFP vows continued presence in Malampaya gas field Philippine News Agency 28 Jan 2026 -- The Armed Forces of the Philippines (AFP) assured the public of its continuous presence in the oil-rich Malampaya gas field, citing its value as "a lifeline" of the country's energy security.

  • Joint Statement of the United States and African Union on the Launch of a Strategic Investment Working Group US Dept. of State 28 Jan 2026 -- The following joint statement was released by the Government of the United States of America and the African Union Commission on the occasion of today's agreement to establish a Strategic Investment Working Group.

  • Secretary of State Marco Rubio Before the Senate Committee on Foreign Relations on U.S. Policy Towards Venezuela US Dept. of State 28 Jan 2026
  • Rubio Lays Out US Policy on Venezuela, NATO, Ukraine and Iran in Senate Testimony Sputnik 28 Jan 2026 -- Secretary of State Marco Rubio discussed Venezuela, NATO, Ukraine and other policy in Senate testimony Wednesday.

Other Conflicts


    Ukraine Conflict News

  • 28 January 2026 - Daily Press Briefing by the Office of the Spokesperson for the Secretary-General United Nations 28 Jan 2026

    • Operation Iron Swords / Operation Al-Aqsa Flood | Operation Northern Arrows | Giddeon's Chariots

  • Netanyahu plans to maintain Israeli 'security control' of Gaza Press TV 28 Jan 2026 -- Benjamin Netanyahu, the prime minister of the Israeli regime, says Tel Aviv will maintain "security control" of Gaza, refusing to allow the establishment of a Palestinian state.

  • Potential turning point for Gaza as peace plan enters second phase: UN envoy UN News 28 Jan 2026 -- The start of a second phase of a stabilisation plan for Gaza offers a potential turning point for the war-ravaged enclave, a senior UN official told the Security Council on Wednesday.
  • Consolidate Gaza Ceasefire, Halt Escalating Violence, Settlement Activity in West Bank, Deputy Special Coordinator Tells Security Council Open Debate UN Security Council 28 Jan 2026 -- As the Security Council gathered the day after the body of the last Israeli hostage in Gaza was retrieved, a United Nations Middle East peace expert stressed the need to consolidate the tenuous ceasefire, while also raising alarm about the escalation of violence and settlements in the occupied West Bank.

  • Demolition of UNRWA headquarters in East Jerusalem: joint statement UK FCDO 28 Jan 2026 -- Joint statement by the foreign ministers of Belgium, Canada, Denmark, France, Iceland, Ireland, Japan, Norway, Portugal, Spain and the UK on demolitions by the Israeli authorities of the UNRWA headquarters in East Jerusalem.
  • The UK remains committed to supporting implementation of Resolution 2803 and forging a peaceful future for Israelis and Palestinians: UK statement at the UN Security Council UK FCDO 28 Jan 2026 -- Statement by Ambassador James Kariuki, UK Chargé d'Affaires to the UN, at the UN Security Council meeting on Gaza.

    • Other Conflicts

  • Today's top news: Occupied Palestinian Territory, Democratic Republic of the Congo, Sudan, Ukraine UNOCHA 28 Jan 2026

  • China moves structure in Yellow Sea border row with Korea Korea.net 28 Jan 2026 -- China has removed a structure that it installed in overlapping waters of the Yellow (West) Sea, but says the move was an independent decision made by a company, not due to Korea's request or diplomatic consultations.

  • House leader: Filipinos back PBBM's firm stand vs. China Philippine News Agency 28 Jan 2026 -- Deputy Speaker Paolo Ortega V of La Union on Wednesday lauded President Ferdinand R. Marcos Jr. for taking a firm, law-based stand in defending Philippine territory, as Filipinos overwhelmingly support efforts to protect the country's rights in the West Philippine Sea.

  • Why Is Islamic State Targeting Chinese Citizens In Afghanistan? RFE/RL 28 Jan 2026 -- The bombing earlier this month on a Chinese hotel restaurant in Kabul, claimed by a regional affiliate of the Islamic State extremist group, has raised new questions about the its operations in Taliban-run Afghanistan and what analysts say is an emerging campaign targeting Chinese citizens.

  • Yemen warns of attacks on US warships in regional waters IRNA 28 Jan 2026 -- Yemen's Ansarullah Movement has warned the United States against military buildup in the West Asia region, saying Sana'a is prepared to confront the aircraft carrier USS Abraham Lincoln in regional waters.
  • Yemen's Ansarullah cautions US against aircraft carrier deployment in Red Sea Press TV 28 Jan 2026 -- The Ansarullah resistance movement has warned the United States against the deployment of the USS Abraham Lincoln aircraft carrier and guided missile destroyers accompanying it in the strategic Red Sea and the Arabian Sea, stressing that Sana'a views such a measure as a threat to Yemen's sovereignty.

  • Russian-Syrian talks President of Russia 28 Jan 2026 -- Vladimir Putin met with President of the Syrian Arab Republic Ahmed al-Sharaa in the Kremlin.
  • Syria: Statement by the Spokesperson on the release of the fifth report of the OPCW investigation and identification team European External Action Service (EEAS) 28 Jan 2026 -- On 20 January 2026, the Organisation for the Prohibition of Chemical Weapons (OPCW) issued a report that found Syrian Arab Air Force responsible for a chemical weapons attack in Kafr Zeita, Syrian Arab Republic on 1 October 2016. The report concludes that there are reasonable grounds to believe that a military helicopter released a cylinder which contained chlorine gas, injuring 35 named individuals and affecting dozens more.

  • Iraq warns of imminent security threats as questions mount over Daesh prisoner escapes in Syria Press TV 28 Jan 2026 -- Iraqi security authorities have warned of imminent and dangerous terrorist movements in the country's western regions, amid growing concerns over cross-border militant activity linked to developments in neighboring Syria.

  • ASEAN affirms 'collective resolve' to find solution to Myanmar crisis Philippine News Agency 28 Jan 2026 -- The Association of Southeast Asian Nations (ASEAN) on Wednesday held an informal meeting on the implementation of the Myanmar peace plan and affirmed its "collective resolve" to find a lasting solution to the crisis in the country.

  • Switzerland joins EU sanctions aimed at curbing gang violence in Haiti Federal Council - Swiss Government 28 Jan 2026 -- The Federal Council is responding to the dramatic situation in Haiti. On 28 January, it took the decision to join the EU sanctions aimed at curbing gang violence. This extends the existing Ordinance on measures concerning Haiti. The new measures come into force at 11pm on 28 January.
  • Visa Restrictions for Haitian Officials Empowering Terrorist Gangs in Haiti US Dept. of State 28 Jan 2026 -- The Department of State continues to promote accountability for those who destabilize Haiti and our region.

  • Facing a critical funding gap, the humanitarian community in DRC is forced to strictly prioritize its response in 2026 UNOCHA 28 Jan 2026 -- The Government of the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) and the humanitarian community in the country today launched an urgent appeal for US$1.4 billion to address humanitarian needs in 2026.

News Reports

  • ICE facility in U.S. California sued for "cruel, inhumane" conditions People's Daily 28 Jan 2026 -- A coalition of legal and immigrant rights organizations in Southern California filed a federal lawsuit in the U.S. District Court in California on Monday, alleging unconstitutional, "cruel and inhumane" conditions at the Adelanto Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) Processing Center.
  • Biden joins former Democratic presidents in denouncing ICE law enforcement controversy; Trump says to de-escalate operations in Minnesota: reports Global Times 28 Jan 2026 -- Former US president Joe Biden joined two former US presidents - Barack Obama and Bill Clinton - in denouncing the law enforcement actions of the Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) in Minnesota where ICE agents shot and killed a second resident this month.
  • 'They are not welcome in Milan,' Italian official denounces ICE's presence at 2026 Winter Olympics as law enforcement continues to draw controversy Global Times 28 Jan 2026 -- The US immigration enforcement agency whose officers have been linked to the fatal shootings of two US citizens in Minneapolis this month is reportedly being deployed in a security support role ahead of the 2026 Winter Olympics in Italy, beginning 6 February — a move that has sparked political backlash and public outrage in Italy, according to media reports.
  • Milan mayor opposes entry of U.S. ICE agents during Winter Olympics People's Daily 28 Jan 2026 -- Milan Mayor Giuseppe Sala on Tuesday said he does not want to see the presence of U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE) agents in Italy during the 2026 Milano-Cortina Winter Olympics.
  • Trump says DHS secretary will not step down amid Democrats' impeachment threats People's Daily 28 Jan 2026 -- U.S. President Donald Trump said Tuesday that Department of Homeland Security (DHS) Secretary Kristi Noem will not step down, even as Democrats threatened to begin impeachment proceedings against her.
  • U.S. federal judge summons ICE chief, warns of contempt over defiance of court orders People's Daily 28 Jan 2026 -- A U.S. federal judge has ordered Todd Lyons, acting director of U.S. Immigration and Customs Enforcement (ICE), to appear in court on Friday to explain why he should not be held in contempt for failing to comply with multiple judicial orders.
  • White House seeks to ease tensions after fatal shooting in Minnesota People's Daily 28 Jan 2026 -- The White House has attempted to ease tensions over the latest fatal shooting of a U.S. citizen by federal immigration officers in Minnesota, even as investigations continue and questions grow among state officials, lawmakers and other groups.

  • Inaugural Meeting of the U.S.-India Drug Policy Executive Working Group India MEA 28 Jan 2026 -- From January 20-21, 2026, the United States hosted the inaugural meeting of the U.S.-India Drug Policy Executive Working Group in Washington, D.C.

  • Telephone conversation with President of the Central African Republic Faustin-Archange Touadera President of Russia 28 Jan 2026 -- Vladimir Putin had a telephone conversation with President of the Central African Republic Faustin-Archange Touadera.
  • Press release on Foreign Minister Sergey Lavrov's meeting with Co-Facilitators for the Intergovernmental Negotiations on UN Security Council Reform Tareq Albanai and Lise Gregoire van Haaren Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 28 Jan 2026
  • Russian State TV Host, Famed Ballet Dancer Hit By New EU Sanctions RFE/RL 28 Jan 2026 -- Several of Russia's most prominent TV personalities, including Pavel Zarubin, a state TV reporter known for fawning reporting on - and nearly unfettered access to - President Vladimir Putin, were targeted in the European Union's newest package of sanctions.
  • Serbia Expects Long-Term Gas Deal With Russia - Vulin Sputnik 28 Jan 2026 -- Serbia is counting on concluding a long-term agreement with Russia on gas supplies, former Serbian Prime Minister and the head of the supervisory board of Srbijagas, Aleksandar Vulin, told Sputnik.
  • The FSB, Lies, And Drunk Texting The FBI: The Curious Case Of Nomma Zarubina RFE/RL 28 Jan 2026 -- Just after 2 p.m. on the first Tuesday of December, in the lower Manhattan courthouse for the US Southern District of New York, a 35-year-old Russian woman and mother of a young daughter stood before a federal judge and pleaded not to be sent to jail.
  • Russia, China pledge to strengthen strategic partnership to counter US influence Press TV 28 Jan 2026 -- Russian and Chinese defense ministers have held a video call, pledging closer cooperation and coordinated action against what they view as Washington's destabilizing influence in Venezuela and Iran.

  • Foreign Ministry Spokesperson Guo Jiakun's Regular Press Conference on January 28, 2026 PRC MofA 28 Jan 2026
  • China, France should jointly inject stability into turbulent world, Wang Yi tells French president's adviser Bonne in phone talk Global Times 28 Jan 2026 -- Wang Yi, a member of the Political Bureau of the Communist Party of China Central Committee and director of the Office of the Central Commission for Foreign Affairs, held a phone call with Emmanuel Bonne, diplomatic adviser to the French President, on Wednesday.
  • Chinese FM spokesperson calls Starmer's China visit 'an opportunity to enhance political mutual trust, deepen practical cooperation' Global Times 28 Jan 2026 -- When asked about expectations on UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer's three-day visit to China and his meeting with Chinese leaders, Chinese FM spokesperson Guo Jiakun said against a backdrop of a turbulent and complex international situation, continuous communication and strengthened cooperation between China and the UK, both permanent members of the UN Security Council, serve the common interests of the peoples, and are conducive to promoting world peace, stability and development.

  • Lai warns opposition: Approve trade pact or risk higher U.S. tariffs CNA 28 Jan 2026 -- President Lai Ching-te (賴清德) on Tuesday urged the Legislature to approve a trade agreement with the United States that has yet to be finalized, warning that a failure to do so could lead the U.S. to hike tariffs on Taiwanese goods to 25 percent or higher.
  • Taiwan, U.S. hold sixth Economic Prosperity Partnership Dialogue CNA 28 Jan 2026 -- Officials from Taiwan and the United States held a sixth round of talks under the Economic Prosperity Partnership Dialogue (EPPD) in Washington, D.C. on Tuesday and signed a pact agreeing to the principles of "Pax Silica."
  • KMT-CCP think tank exchange to promote cross-strait engagement: KMT CNA 28 Jan 2026 -- The Kuomintang (KMT) confirmed Wednesday it will send a delegation to Beijing from Feb. 2‑4 for the first official think tank exchange with the Chinese Communist Party (CCP), aiming to foster constructive cross-strait interaction.
  • Peaceful reunification to bring opportunities to Taiwan: mainland spokesperson People's Daily 28 Jan 2026 -- A Chinese mainland spokesperson said on Wednesday that peaceful reunification will create tremendous opportunities for Taiwan's economic and social development.
  • With the strong backing of the motherland, every aspect of Taiwan including infrastructure would improve significantly: mainland spokesperson Global Times 28 Jan 2026 -- Peaceful reunification would create enormous opportunities for Taiwan's socioeconomic development and with the strong backing of the motherland, every aspect of Taiwan region including infrastructure construction, would improve significantly, Zhang Han, a spokesperson for the State Council Taiwan Affairs Office, told a press conference on Wednesday, in response to a question regarding heated discussion on the island of Taiwan on news that the mainland's high-speed rail operating mileage has exceeded 50,000 kilometers.
  • Mainland spokesperson slams Hsieh Chang-ting's pro-Japan remarks, says they once again expose the servile, traitorous nature of DPP politicians Global Times 28 Jan 2026 -- Japan bears historical responsibility to the Chinese people on the Taiwan question, having committed innumerable heinous crimes during its 50 years of colonial rule over the Taiwan region. The recent remarks of Hsieh Chang-ting, head of the "Taiwan-Japan Relations Association," disregard history and betray their roots, once again exposing the servile and traitorous nature of Democratic Progressive Party (DPP) politicians who grovel to external forces in exchange for their support, Zhang Han, a spokesperson for the State Council Taiwan Affairs Office, said on Wednesday.
  • Japan in no position to interfere in affairs of China's Taiwan: FM spokesperson People's Daily 28 Jan 2026 -- A Chinese foreign ministry spokesperson said on Tuesday that the Japanese side is in no position to interfere in the affairs of China's Taiwan region, either from the perspective of history or law.
  • PLA activities in the waters and airspace around Taiwan ROC MND 28 Jan 2026

  • Wife of S. Korea's ex-president Yoon sentenced to 20 months in prison for bribery People's Daily 28 Jan 2026 -- Kim Keon-hee, wife of the impeached former South Korean President Yoon Suk-yeol, was sentenced to 20 months in prison for taking bribes, live footage showed Wednesday.

  • Trump Calls On Iran To 'Make A Deal' Or Next US Attack Will Be 'Far Worse' RFE/RL 28 Jan 2026 -- US President Donald Trump has reiterated his call for Iran to "make a deal" over its nuclear program or face a "far worse attack" than the one aimed at the country last year when US and Israeli air strikes pummeled Iranian nuclear sites.
  • Trump says another "armada" sailing toward Iran People's Daily 28 Jan 2026 -- U.S. President Donald Trump said Tuesday that an additional "armada" of U.S. military vessels is sailing towards Iran, expressing hope that Iran will agree to a deal.
  • Trump Asserts 'Massive Armada With Great Power' Racing Toward Iran Sputnik 28 Jan 2026 -- US President Donald Trump said on Wednesday that a "massive armada" containing great power is speeding toward Iran.
  • Iran 'Practically Naked' If US Attacks, But Can Retaliate With Force RFE/RL 28 Jan 2026 -- Iran is bracing for a potential attack as the United States deploys key military assets, including an air carrier and additional bombers, to the Middle East.
  • Enemy will suffer severe damage in potential war with Iran: Cmdr. IRNA 28 Jan 2026 -- Deputy Chief of the Iranian Army for Coordination Rear Admiral Habibollah Sayyari has reiterated the preparedness of the Iranian Army to counter any threat against the country, noting that in case of any war, the enemy will suffer "severe damage."
  • IRGC: Iran holds upper hand in determining any war's endgame Press TV 28 Jan 2026 -- The Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC) says Iran holds the upper hand in determining the outcome of any war, asserting that applying military pressure against the Islamic Republic has yielded no results.
  • Iran's power makes any hostile military action 'high-risk': IRGC deputy cmdr. Press TV 28 Jan 2026 -- Iran's defense advancements have fundamentally altered the strategic landscape, making the cost-benefit analysis of any military aggression against the country "extremely complex and high-risk" for adversaries, says the deputy commander-in-chief of the Islamic Revolution Guards Corps (IRGC).
  • Senior commander: Iran fully ready, any adventurism will be costly for enemy Press TV 28 Jan 2026 -- A senior Iranian commander says Iran's enemies have failed militarily and are now waging hybrid and cognitive warfare, stressing that any hostile move against the country would cause serious damage and heavy costs for the adversaries.
  • 'Fingers on trigger': Iran warns of strong response while signaling openness to 'fair' deal Press TV 28 Jan 2026 -- Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi of Iran issued a warning on Wednesday, saying the Armed Forces of the Islamic Republic are prepared to respond "immediately and powerfully" to any aggression, even as he signaled Tehran's willingness to reach a nuclear deal under the right conditions.
  • Iran, Saudi Arabia warn of consequences of regional escalation amid US threats IRNA 28 Jan 2026 -- Foreign ministers of Iran and Saudi Arabia have warned against the repercussions of any regional escalation amid the dispatch of US warships to the region.
  • Iran, Saudi Arabia warn of 'dangerous consequences' of regional escalation Press TV 28 Jan 2026 -- Iran and Saudi Arabia have warned of the "dangerous consequences" of any escalation of tensions for security and stability in West Asia, as the US military buildup near Iran continues.
  • Negotiations with US under military threats not 'useful': Iran FM IRNA 28 Jan 2026 -- Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has rejected talks with the United States as long as bullying and dictates persist, saying diplomacy imposed through military threats cannot be effective or useful.
  • Even a limited US strike will bring regretful response: Iran Press TV 28 Jan 2026 -- Iran says it will react decisively to even a limited US attack as it dismisses rumors of back-channel talks amid escalating threats of a potential military confrontation between the two countries.
  • FM Araghchi: Negotiations cannot work under threats Press TV 28 Jan 2026 -- Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi has warned that imposing diplomacy through military threats cannot be effective or productive, saying that no decision has yet been made regarding negotiations with the United States.
  • Iranian Missiles Can't Reach US, But Can Reach US Bases in Mideast - Senior Diplomat Sputnik 28 Jan 2026 -- The range of Iranian missiles is not enough to reach the United States, but it is enough to reach US bases in the Middle East region, Iranian Deputy Foreign Minister Kazem Gharibabadi said on Wednesday.
  • Iran Ready For Dialogue With US, But Will Defend Itself if Pushed - Mission to UN Sputnik 28 Jan 2026 -- Tehran is open to negotiations but will defend itself if provoked, Iran's Mission to the UN said on Wednesday, responding to remarks by US President Donald Trump that US warships are heading toward Iran.
  • UN mission: Iran to give unprecedented response to any US attack IRNA 28 Jan 2026 -- Iranian mission to the United Nations has warned against US President Donald Trump's act of adventurism, saying that Iran is ready to negotiate, but if had to, it will defend itself like never before.
  • Iran's nuclear chief urges Grossi to stick to his IAEA mandate after NPT jab Press TV 28 Jan 2026 -- The head of the Atomic Energy Organization of Iran (AEOI) has cautioned the Director General of the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) against using threatening language, urging him to act within the legal boundaries of his mandate.
  • Iran expects Grossi to fulfil his responsibilities under IAEA statute: Nuclear chief IRNA 28 Jan 2026 -- The head of the Atomic Energy Organization of Iran (AEOI) has urged the Director General of the International Atomic Energy Agency (IAEA) not to use threatening language toward the Islamic Republic, saying Tehran expects Rafael Grossi to act within the scope of his legal duties.
  • UN urges diplomacy, restraint toward Iran IRNA 28 Jan 2026 -- The United Nations has reiterated that diplomacy and dialogue remain the best approach toward Iran, calling on all parties to exercise restraint amid rising regional tensions.
  • Saudi Arabia to Deny Airspace for Attacks Against Iran - Reports Sputnik 28 Jan 2026 -- Riyadh has assured Tehran that it will not allow its airspace and territory to be used for attacks against Iran, a Saudi news agency reported.
  • Tajikistan calls for peaceful resolution of conflicts between Iran and US IRNA 28 Jan 2026 -- The government of Tajikistan has expressed serious concern over the escalating tensions between Iran and the United States, calling for the peaceful resolution of all conflicts only through diplomacy.
  • Iraqi foreign minister says Iran is ready for talks but US has yet to respond IRNA 28 Jan 2026 -- Iraqi Foreign Minister Fuad Hussein says Iran has announced its readiness for dialogue with the United States, but it has yet to receive any response from Washington.
  • War could reach your own doorsteps: Iraqi anti-terror group warns Iran's enemies Press TV 28 Jan 2026 -- The secretary-general of Iraq's Harakat al-Nujaba anti-terror group has warned Iran's adversaries about the inevitably far-reaching consequences of any fresh aggression that the enemies could stage against the Islamic Republic.
  • Iraq's Nujaba warns will not hesitate to defend Iran in case of any attack on Islamic Republic Press TV 28 Jan 2026 -- An Iraqi anti-terror group has vowed that the Arab country's resistance forces would join the response to any attack on Iran, warning that any aggressive action against the Islamic Republic would drag Iraq and the Persian Gulf states into a broader regional conflict.
  • France Backs EU Terror Listing Of Iran's IRGC, Clearing Path For Unified Bloc Stance RFE/RL 28 Jan 2026 -- France has announced that it now supports the designation of Iran's Islamic Revolutionary Guards Corps (IRGC) as a terrorist organization, marking a major policy shift that could clear the way for European Union consensus on the issue.
  • Foreign Minister Espen Barth Eide strongly condemns the brutal use of force in Iran Government of Norway 28 Jan 2026 -- 'I strongly condemn the brutal and disproportionate use of force inflicted on the civilian population by the Iranian authorities in connection with the protests in Iran', said Eide.
  • Brooklyn Man Sentenced to 15 Years in Prison for Murder-for-Hire Plot and Stalking a Journalist and Prominent Critic of the Iranian Government US Dept. of Justice 28 Jan 2026 -- Today, convicted murderer Carlisle Rivera, also known as "Pop," was sentenced to 15 years in prison for his participation in a murder-for-hire plot directed by the Government of Iran targeting Masih Alinejad, a journalist, author, and human rights activist.
  • Man convicted of spying for Mossad, assisting Israel's failed sabotage plot executed in Iran Press TV 28 Jan 2026 -- A man convicted of espionage for Israel's spy agency Mossad has been executed in Iran following the completion of legal procedures and confirmation of the verdict by the Supreme Court, according to the Judiciary.

  • Iraq's former PM Maliki rejects 'blatant US interference' after Trump warning Press TV 28 Jan 2026 -- Former Iraqi Prime Minister Nouri al-Maliki has dismissed "blatant US interference" in the country's domestic affairs, a day after US President Donald Trump warned that Washington would withdraw its support for Baghdad if the veteran politician returns to power.
  • Trump threatens to "no longer help Iraq" if Iraq's ex-PM regains power People's Daily 28 Jan 2026 -- U.S. President Donald Trump threatened on Tuesday that the United States would "no longer help Iraq" if former Iraqi Prime Minister Nuri Kamal al-Maliki returns to power.
  • Trump threatens Iraq over ex-PM Maliki's return Press TV 28 Jan 2026 -- US President Donald Trump has said Washington would end its "support" for Iraq if former prime minister Nouri al-Maliki was reinstated, issuing his strongest warning yet over the composition of Iraq's next government.

  • Courtesy Call on State Minister for Foreign Affairs HORII by H.E. Mr. Yosef AMRANI, Political Director, Ministry of Forein Affairs of the State of Israel Ministry of Foreign Affairs of Japan 28 Jan 2026

  • 3rd India-Saudi Arabia Security Working Group India MEA 28 Jan 2026 -- The 3rd meeting of India-Saudi Arabia Security Working Group under the Political, Consular and Security Cooperation Committee of Strategic Partnership Council was held on 28 January 2026 in Riyadh.
  • Transcript of Special Briefing on the State visit of President of EU Council & President of EU Commission to India (January 27, 2026) India MEA 28 Jan 2026

  • Press conference - Dili, Timor-Leste Prime Minister of Australia 28 Jan 2026

  • Foreign Ministry Spokeswoman Maria Zakharova's answer to a media question on the US administration's plans to tighten the blockade of Cuba Russia Ministry of Foreign Affairs 28 Jan 2026

  • Minister for Foreign Affairs receives German counterpart Government Offices of Sweden 28 Jan 2026 -- On 26 January, Minister for Foreign Affairs Maria Malmer Stenergard received Germany's Federal Minister for Foreign Affairs, Dr Johann Wadephul, in Stockholm.

  • Budrys: Time for Europe to sleep on a cloud is over Lithuania MFA 28 Jan 2026 -- On 28 January, the Minister of Foreign Affairs, Kęstutis Budrys, met with the ambassadors of the member states of the European Union to Lithuania.

  • Slovak PM describes Trump's state of mind as 'dangerous' to EU leaders Press TV 28 Jan 2026 -- Slovakia's Prime Minister Robert Fico has privately warned EU leaders that US President Donald Trump's state of mind appeared "dangerous" during a recent meeting at Mar-a-Lago.

  • Remarks by Minister of Foreign Affairs of Armenia Ararat Mirzoyan at the session of the Parliamentary Assembly of the Council of Europe Ministry of Foreign Affairs of the Republic of Armenia 28 Jan 2026
  • In recent years, the impossible has been done in many directions, the impossible has become a reality: the Prime Minister attends the solemn session of the Ministry of Defense Prime Minister of the Republic of Armenia 28 Jan 2026 -- On the occasion of the 34th anniversary of the formation of the Armed Forces of the Republic of Armenia, Prime Minister Nikol Pashinyan visited the Ministry of Defense and participated in the solemn session.

  • World News in Brief: UN Support Office in Haiti, Goodwill Ambassador Theo James in Syria, urgent appeal for millions in DR Congo UN News 28 Jan 2026 -- The Security Council-endorsed UN Support Office in Haiti is on track to deliver on behalf of the Haitian people, UN Spokesperson Stéphane Dujarric said on Wednesday.
